mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy, which lowers discount rates and boosts equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 6% indicates weakening labor market conditions, which may signal economic slowdown and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods of UNRATE > 6% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals robust economic expansion, which typically drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods of rising GDPC1 and INDPRO are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer demand, as indicated by rising RSAFS (consumer credit) and housing starts (HOUST), correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days due to increased corporate revenue and investor confidence.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods of rising RSAFS and HOUST are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects increased manufacturing activity, which typically leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days due to improved economic outlook and corporate earnings.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods of rising INDPRO are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a scenario where equity valuations may be negatively impacted due to uncertainty, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods of high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 900 thousand units signals weak demand for residential construction, which may lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days due to reduced economic activity.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods of HOUST < 900 are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A tight labor market, indicated by low unemployment (UNRATE) and strong job growth (PAYEMS), often leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days due to increased consumer spending and corporate investment.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods of low UNRATE and high PAYEMS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.
